translations.text: Attired in silk and brocade, they shall be arrayed face to face.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Dressed in Sundus and Istabraq, facing each other,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Attired in silk and silk embroidery, facing one another.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: dressed in fine silk and brocade, sitting face to face.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: dressed in silks and brocade, set face to face.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Wearing [garments of] fine silk and brocade, facing each other.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: clothed in fine silk and rich brocade, sitting face to face with one another.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They shall wear of fine and thick silk, (sitting) face to face;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: dressed in fine silk and in rich brocade, and they will face each other:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Dressed in fine silk and in rich brocade, they will face each other;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Yalbasoona min sundusin waistabraqin mutaqabileena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Just like that. We shall pair them with companions with large black eyes.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So it is; and We have wedded them to maidens with gorgeous eyes.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Even so; and We shall espouse them to wide-eyed houris,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Even so. And We shall pair them with fair damsels large- eyed.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So (it will be), and We shall marry them to Houris (female fair ones) with wide, lovely eyes.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So it is, and We will wed them to lovely companions.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Thus shall it be: and We shall espouse them to fair, wide-eyed maidens.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So (it will be). And We shall marry them to Hur (fair females) with wide lovely eyes,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Even so (it will be). And We shall wed them unto fair ones with wide, lovely eyes.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So shall it be, and We shall wed them to black-eyed houris.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: As such, We shall wed them to wideeyed houris (the virgins of Paradise).

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Thus. And We will marry them to fair women with large, [beautiful] eyes.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We shall unite them to maidens with big black and white lovely eyes.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Thus (shall it be), and We will wed them with Houris pure, beautiful ones.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: so it will be. We shall wed them to maidens with large, dark eyes.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So; and We shall join them to fair women with beautiful, big, and lustrous eyes.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Kathalika wazawwajnahum bihoorin AAeenin

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They will call for every kind of fruit with satisfaction.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: In it they will ask for all kinds of fruit, with safety.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: therein calling for every fruit, secure.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They will call therein for every kind of fruit in security.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They will call therein for every kind of fruit in peace and security;

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They will call therein for every kind of fruit, in peace and security.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: While resting in security, they shall call for all kinds of fruit.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They will call therein for every kind of fruit in peace and security;

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They call therein for every fruit in safety.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Secure [from any kind of harm,] there they will call for every kind of fruit [they wish].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: There in security, they will call for every kind of fruit.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They will call therein for every [kind of] fruit - safe and secure.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will be offered all kinds of fruits, in peace and security.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They shall call therein for every fruit in security;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They will call therein for every kind of fruit, in peace and security.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: There can they call for every kind of fruit in peace and security;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: YadAAoona feeha bikulli fakihatin amineena

56.arabic: لَا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا ٱلْمَوْتَ إِلَّا ٱلْمَوْتَةَ ٱلْأُولَىٰ ۖ وَوَقَىٰهُمْ عَذَابَ ٱلْجَحِيمِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: There they will not know any death apart from the first death they had died, and will be kept safe from the torment of Hell

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They will not taste death again in it, except their former death; and Allah has saved them from the punishment of fire.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They shall not taste therein of death, save the first death, And He shall guard them against the chastisement of Hell --

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They will not taste death therein, except the first death; and He will preserve them from the torment of the Flaming Fire.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They will never taste death therein except the first death (of this world), and He will save them from the torment of the blazing Fire,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Therein they will not taste death, beyond the first death; and He will protect them from the torment of Hell.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They shall not taste death except the death in this world. And Allah will save them from the chastisement of Hell

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They will never taste death therein except the first death, and He will save them from the torment of the blazing Fire,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They taste not death therein, save the first death. And He hath saved them from the doom of hell,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Other than the first death, they will not taste death therein, and He will save them from the punishment of hell

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: There they shall not taste death, except the first death, and He will shield them from the punishment of Hell,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They will not taste death therein except the first death, and He will have protected them from the punishment of Hellfire

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will not experience any death other than that which they have already been through.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They shall not taste therein death except the first death, and He will save them from the punishment of the hell,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They will not taste death therein, save the first death. God will save them from the torment of Hell

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nor will they there taste Death, except the first death; and He will preserve them from the Penalty of the Blazing Fire,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: La yathooqoona feeha almawta illa almawtata aloola wawaqahum AAathaba aljaheemi

57.arabic: فَضْلًا مِّن رَّبِّكَ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: By the beneficence of your Lord. This will be the great success.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: By the munificence of your Lord; this is the great success.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: a bounty from thy Lord; that is the mighty triumph.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: A bounty from thy Lord. That! that is the supreme achievement.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: As a Bounty from your Lord! That will be the supreme success!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: A favor from your Lord. That is the supreme salvation.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: as a favour from your Lord. That is the great triumph.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: As a bounty from your Lord! That will be the supreme success!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: A bounty from thy Lord. That is the supreme triumph.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: —a grace from your Lord. That is the great success.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: as a bounty from your Lord. That will be the mighty triumph.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: As bounty from your Lord. That is what is the great attainment.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God will protect them from the torment of hell as a favor from your Lord. (Muhammad), this is certainly the greatest triumph.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: A grace from your Lord; this is the great achievement.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: as an act of grace. That will be the supreme triumph.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: As a Bounty from thy Lord! that will be the supreme achievement!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fadlan min rabbika thalika huwa alfawzu alAAatheemu

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Therefore We have made this (Qur'an) easy in your tongue. They may haply take a warning.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We have made this Qur’an easy in your language, for them to understand.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Now We have made it easy by thy tongue, that haply they may remember.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We have made it easy in thy language; they might haply be admonished.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Certainly, We have made this (Quran) easy in your tongue, in order that they may remember.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We made it easy in your language, so that they may remember.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (O Prophet), We have made this Book easy in your tongue so that they may take heed.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Certainly, We have made this easy in your tongue, in order that they may remember.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We have made (this Scripture) easy in thy language only that they may heed.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed We have made it simple in your language, so that they may take admonition.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We have now made it easy on your tongue, in order that they remember.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And indeed, We have eased the Qur'an in your tongue that they might be reminded.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We have made the Quran easy for you to recite so that perhaps they may take heed.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So have We made it easy in your tongue that they may be mindful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We have made this Quran easy to understand, in your own language, so that they may take heed.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Verily, We have made this (Qur'an) easy, in thy tongue, in order that they may give heed.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fainnama yassarnahu bilisanika laAAallahum yatathakkaroona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So you wait. They are also waiting.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore wait (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) – they too are waiting.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So be on the watch; they too are on the watch.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wait thou then; verily they also are waiting.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Wait then (O Muhammad SAW); Verily, they (too) are waiting.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So wait and watch. They too are waiting and watching.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Wait, then; they too are waiting.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Wait then; verily, they (too) are waiting.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Wait then (O Muhammad). Lo! they (too) are waiting.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So wait! They [too] are waiting.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: So be watchful, they too are watching.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So watch, [O Muhammad]; indeed, they are watching [for your end].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Wait (for God's decree) and they too will be waiting.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Therefore wait; surely they are waiting.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Wait then; they too are waiting.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So wait thou and watch; for they (too) are waiting.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fairtaqib innahum murtaqiboona
